title: pseudopanax crassifolius var. pentadactylus
intro: Pseudopanax crassifolius var. pentadactylus, commonly known as Five Finger, is a variety of the Pseudopanax crassifolius plant, native to New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Pseudopanax crassifolius var. pentadactylus include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as promoting wound healing and immune system function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in topical applications such as creams and ointments.
